data.title: Andrew Peller Ltd. will release Q1 2026 results on August 6, 2025, with a conference call on August 7, 2025 at 10:00 am ET.
data.description: Andrew Peller Limited (ADW.A / ADW.B) based in Grimsby, Ontario, will be releasing its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025, on August 6, 2025, post market closure. A conference call is scheduled by management to delve into these financial figures on August 7, 2025, at 10:00 a.m. (ET), along with a Q&A session thereafter.

data.title: Labour MP criticizes Nigel Farage's party for accepting crypto donations, calling for ban on political donations in crypto.
data.description: Last month, Nigel Farage's Reform UK party began accepting cryptocurrency donations. Now, a Labour Member of Parliament is advocating for the banning of this practice.

data.title: Trump's harsh words for Powell scrutinize Fed decisions. Rates likely steady, with potential cut in September.
data.description: Interest rates are expected to remain unchanged by the central bank this month. However, there is a possibility of a rate cut as soon as September.

data.title: Netflix stock falls after modest Q2 beat, analysts predict stronger second half of 2025 with big content releases.
data.description: Analysts forecasting Netflix's performance have shifted their focus beyond the company's slightly better-than-expected results in the second quarter, envisioning a more lucrative second half of 2025. Despite a decline in Netflix’s stock, experts view the recent quarter as a prelude to a significant release period for new content.

data.title: Sarepta Therapeutics stock crashes after third gene therapy patient death, wiping out gains, causing concerns.
data.description: On Friday, there was a significant decline in Sarepta's stock, resulting in the loss of nearly all of the gains related to its restructuring, following the death of a third patient who had received one of its gene therapies. This news caused a crash in Sarepta Therapeutics stock, impacting its market value. Investors experienced a substantial drop in their investments as the company faced the unfortunate event of losing another recipient of its gene therapy treatment. The incident highlighted the risks associated with Sarepta's gene therapy program and raised concerns about its safety and effectiveness. This development had a major impact on the company's financial performance and reputation in the market.

data.title: Union Pacific considers acquiring Norfolk Southern. CSX stock rises. BNSF expansion suggested.
data.description: Could Berkshire Hathaway's railroad subsidiary, BNSF, be considering a coast-to-coast expansion? Union Pacific is reportedly exploring a potential deal with Norfolk Southern, causing a spike in CSX stock. This move comes amidst increasing competition and shifting dynamics in the railway industry. Stay tuned for updates on this developing story.

data.title: Edward M. Kennedy Institute to give Lifetime Achievement Award to President Biden at 10th anniversary celebration in Boston. Former Labor Secretary and Boston Mayor M.J. Walsh, Retired Admiral L. Franchetti to also receive leadership awards.
data.description: Leadership awards will be given out at the 10th-anniversary celebration, with former Labor Secretary and Boston Mayor Martin J. Walsh, along with retired Admiral Lisa Franchetti, being the recipients. The awards, which will be presented at the Edward M. Kennedy Institute in Boston on July 18, 2025, aim to recognize and celebrate inspired leadership. This event marks an important milestone for the institute and highlights the achievements of these distinguished individuals.

data.title: Global preservative-free eye drops market to reach US$1.67 billion by 2031, with a CAGR of 4.3%. - The Insight Partners.
data.description: On July 18, 2025, a recent report by The Insight Partners revealed substantial growth in the global preservative-free eye drops market due to the rising occurrence of ocular diseases. The market is projected to witness significant expansion in the coming years, with expected growth reaching impressive numbers.

data.title: One year after CrowdStrike outages, traveler concerns over flight disruptions at all-time high, leading to thousands of delays and cancellations worldwide.
data.description: In St. Petersburg, Florida, on July 18, 2025, it will have been a year since the CrowdStrike outage that resulted in numerous flight delays and cancellations globally. This incident was part of a series of disruptions travelers have encountered in the past year. The outage impacted thousands of flights and caused inconvenience for many passengers, highlighting the significance of technological vulnerabilities in the airline industry.
